project_type: Python AI Agent System - Multi-agent orchestrator with multi-provider LLM support
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, no Makefile or complex build system
test_framework: No formal test framework in place. Manual testing via REPL interaction.
test_command: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'J.A.R.V.I.S_system'); from core import orchestrator, model_router; from core.providers import ollama_provider, groq_provider; from core.agents import coder_agent, researcher_agent; from core.memory import conversation; from core.tools import file_tools, python_executor; print('All imports OK')"
verification_instructions: 1. Install requirements_v2.txt. 2. Verify all modules import cleanly without errors. 3. Run python -m py_compile on each .py file to verify syntax. 4. Run run_jarvis_v2.py with --help or quick exit to verify entry point works.
environment_constraints: Python 3.9.25 in sandbox. Code must be Python 3.8+ compatible. Target hardware: Windows, AMD Ryzen 5 5500U (CPU only inference), 16GB RAM. Ollama may not be installed in sandbox - code must handle graceful fallback. No GPU available.
contribution_requirements: Comments in Bahasa Indonesia for non-technical explanations, English for docstrings. Type hints required. Production quality code with proper error handling. Must work offline with Ollama and online with Groq independently.
key_patterns: Current code uses Phidata framework with Nvidia NIM API. New code replaces this with raw OpenAI SDK for multi-provider compatibility. Interactive REPL pattern with special commands (help, tools, clear, exit). Self-healing loop pattern for autonomous coding. File tools and Python executor are core capabilities.
directory_structure: PRE-AIAgentJarvis/ is the repo root. J.A.R.V.I.S_system/ contains the main agent code. New code goes in J.A.R.V.I.S_system/core/ subfolder. Old files remain untouched.
